The data presented in the table provides a comprehensive overview of the amplification and analysis of sequences related to the
Coronaviridae family. Specifically, the metrics outlined demonstrate the efficiency and resource utilization associated with
targeted sequencing efforts. For instance, the “No. of target seqs.” column indicates a total of 547 sequences designated for
amplification, which reflects the extensive scope of the study aimed at identifying and characterizing viral variants.
Additionally, the “Time against input fasta database” metric shows an efficient processing time of 1:29, suggesting that the
chosen methodologies facilitate rapid analysis without compromising accuracy. The table also highlights the “RAM used (MB)
against input fasta” at 64±03, along with the “No. of primers” utilized, which stands at 64. This data underscores the balance
between computational resource demands and the volume of data processed, reinforcing the effectiveness of the employed
protocols in handling large-scale genomic analyses. Collectively, these insights contribute to the ongoing research efforts in
understanding the complexities of coronaviruses and their implications in public health.
